exercises.name: Kettlebell Swing
exercises.description:
1.When performing a Kettlebell swing, you want to ensure back safety. Keep your back slightly arched in the lower position rather than letting it hunch forward. 

2. You want to drive from your lower body. Ensure that you’re not pulling too much with your shoulders and arms.

3. Place the Kettlebell between your feet. Push back with your butt and bend your knees to get into the starting position. 

4.Swing the Kettlebell between your legs forcefully. Quickly reverse the direction and drive through with your hips taking the Kettlebell straight out to chest level. 

5.Let the Kettlebell swing back between your legs.

6.Repeat

exercises.name: Kettlebell T-Rex Swing
exercises.description:
Hold the kettlebell with both hands and perform a swing by pushing your hips back and thrusting forward. 

This motion mimics a T-Rex’s short arms swinging. Focuses on hips, glutes, and shoulders.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Turkish Get Up
exercises.description:
1.Lie on the floor with a kettlebell straight up over your chest, palms up and one leg bent at the hip and knee.

2.Thrust the bent leg forward raising your upper body off the floor, placing your hand into the side.

3.Come up onto one knee with the kettlebell overhead and your arm straight.

4.Stand upright with your arm fully extended overhead. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Upright Row
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding one kettlebell in both hands in front with your arms straight.

2.Raise the kettlebell up to just below your chin, keeping your elbows above your hands. Keep the kettlebell close to your body.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Walking Lunges
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in each hand at your sides. Lunge forward with one leg, then step through with the other leg to perform a walking lunge. 

This works legs, glutes, and core.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Windmill
exercises.description:
1) Clean and press a kettlebell overhead with one arm. 

2) Keeping the kettlebell locked out at all times, push your hip out in the direction of the locked out kettlebell.

3) Turn your feet out at a forty-five degree angle from the arm with the kettlebell. 

4) Lower yourself until you can touch the floor in the opposite direction. 

5) Pause for a second and then reverse the motion back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Windmill to Press
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand overhead with your arm fully extended. 

Keep your gaze on the kettlebell as you hinge at the hips and slide the opposite hand down your leg toward the floor, keeping your core tight and your arm extended overhead. 

Push through your hips to return to standing, then press the kettlebell overhead if it isn’t already fully extended. Lower the kettlebell back to chest level and repeat continuously.

exercises.name: Key to a Smith Machine RDL #shorts
exercises.description: Key to a Smith Machine RDL #shorts

exercises.name: Kick Throughs
exercises.description: Start in a bear crawl position. Kick one leg through to the opposite side, rotating your torso. Alternate sides.

exercises.name: Kipping Knees to Elbows
exercises.description:
1. Start by hanging from a pull up bar with hands about shoulder-width apart. 

2. With feet together, slowly raise both of your knees up towards your upper body, pulling with straight arms, until your knees contact your elbows at the top. 

3. Return to the start position under control before moving on to the next rep.

exercises.name: Kipping Muscle Up
exercises.description:
1. Start by hanging on a bar with hands at shoulder-width.

2. Slightly lean back as you pull the bar towards your torso, bringing yourself as high as possible. 

3. Once the bar contacts your body, lean forward and rotate the elbows upwards. 

4. Push yourself upwards away from the bar, until your arms are fully locked and you are over top of the bar.

exercises.name: Kipping Pull Up
exercises.description:
1. Start by gripping a pull up bar with a grip slightly wider than shoulder-width. 

2. Initiate the movement with the shoulders, pushing your body into an arched back position. 

3. From the arched position, lift your hips and legs straight up towards the bar, pulling yourself upwards at the same time. 

4. Pull yourself up until your chin is above the bar, then push yourself back down to the start position for the next rep.

exercises.name: Kipping Toes to Bar
exercises.description:
1. Start by gripping a pull up bar around shoulder-width. 

2. Initiate the movement by swinging forwards from the shoulders while arching your back.

3. Kick your legs forwards and lift your feet towards the bar while pressing into the bar with straight arms. 

3. Contact the bar with both of your feet before swinging back towards the arched back position to repeat.

exercises.name: Knee to Elbow Crunch
exercises.description:
1)  Place your hands by your head. 

2) Bring your knees towards your elbows and your elbows towards your knees. 

3) Shorten the distance between your pelvis and your rib cage as much as possible.

exercises.name: Knee Tuck to Push Up
exercises.description:
Begin in a plank position with hands under shoulders and body forming a straight line from head to heels. While maintaining the plank, explosively tuck both knees toward the chest, lifting the feet slightly off the ground. 

Return to the plank position and immediately perform a push-up, lowering the chest toward the floor and pressing back up to full extension.

exercises.name: Knee Tucks Off Bench
exercises.description: Knee Tucks Off Bench

exercises.name: Knee Up Crunch
exercises.description:
1) Lie on your back with your knees up at a 90-degree angle.  Hands behind your head, light fingertip pressure only. 

2) Exhale and contract your abs, as you slowly lift your shoulder blades off the floor.  

3) Press your low back into the floor and think about pulling your belly button to your spine. Pause at the top of the movement. 

4) Inhale and slowly control your body back to the start position. 

5) Perform this exercise very slowly to avoid using momentum to help.  Your body should remain perfectly still during the movement.

exercises.name: Kneel to Stand to Squat
exercises.description:
Begin in a kneeling position with knees hip-width apart and core engaged. Drive through your feet and hips to rise to a standing position while maintaining balance and control. 

From standing, immediately lower into a squat, keeping your chest upright, core braced, and knees tracking over your toes. 

Press through your heels to return to standing, then return to kneeling to repeat the sequence continuously.

exercises.name: Kneeling alt SA press / narrow press
Kneeling Push Up
exercises.description: Similar to the push-up v2, but performed from a kneeling position to reduce the intensity while still targeting the chest and arms.

exercises.name: Kneeling Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Support your body on your knees and hands with your elbows bent, legs crossed, feet raised and your chest nearly touching the floor.

2.Push up to a straight arm position.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Kneeling RB straight arm pull downs
Kneeling Shoulder Taps
exercises.description: In a tabletop position, tap each shoulder with the opposite hand while keeping your core stable and hips level.

exercises.name: Kneeling Side Plank
exercises.description:
1. Start by lying on the mat on your side, with your body propped up on the elbow and knee of the bottom leg. 

2. Drive your hips up off the mat, and push your hips forward until they are fully extended. 

3. Hold this position, with your top leg fully extended and abdominals tense. 

4. Continue to breathe and hold this position.

5. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Kneeling Stability Ball Rollout to Plank Saw
exercises.description:
Kneel on the floor with a stability ball in front of you, hands on the ball. Roll the ball forward, extending your arms and torso into a plank position while keeping your core engaged. 

From the plank, perform a “saw” motion by slowly rocking your body back and forth over your hands, maintaining a straight line from shoulders to knees. 

Pull the ball back to return to the starting kneeling position and repeat.

exercises.name: Lacrosse Ball Calf Release
exercises.description:
1. Don't roll over any bony protrusions of the ankle or any joints, keep the ball on the muscle. 

2. Start seated, and place the ball just behind your lower leg and above the ankle - using your bodyweight, apply gentle pressure to the ball. 

3. Gently work your way around the calves, in behind your knee, and above your ankle. Move at about 1 inch per second while trying to relax the muscle.

4. If you roll over a spot that is particularly tender or sore, stay on it for 10-20 seconds while trying to relax the area, then move on. 

5. Ensure that you are only feeling a gentle discomfort, and not a serious or "sharp" pain.

exercises.name: Lacrosse Ball Glute Release
exercises.description:
1. Don't roll over any bony protrusions of the spine or any joints, keep the ball on the muscle. 

2. Start seated, and place the ball on your glute just below the lower back - using your bodyweight, apply gentle pressure to the ball. 

3. Gently work your way around the glutes, in below your back. Move at about 1 inch per second while trying to relax the muscle.

4. If you roll over a spot that is particularly tender or sore, stay on it for 10-20 seconds while trying to relax the area, then move on. 

5. Ensure that you are only feeling a gentle discomfort, and not a serious or "sharp" pain.

exercises.name: Lacrosse Ball Hamstring Release
exercises.description:
1. Don't roll over any bony protrusions of the knee or any joints, keep the ball on the muscle. 

2. Start seated on the mat, and place the ball just behind your knee and below your glutes - using your bodyweight, apply gentle pressure to the ball. 

3. Gently work your way around the hamstrings, in below the knee. Move at about 1 inch per second while trying to relax the muscle.

4. If you roll over a spot that is particularly tender or sore, stay on it for 10-20 seconds while trying to relax the area, then move on. 

5. Ensure that you are only feeling a gentle discomfort, and not a serious or "sharp" pain.

exercises.name: Lacrosse Ball Hip Flexor Release
exercises.description:
1. Don't roll over any bony protrusions of the pelvis or any joints, keep the ball on the muscle. 

2. Lie on your chest, and place the ball just below your pelvis and below the hip crease - using your bodyweight, apply gentle pressure to the ball. 

3. Gently work your way around the hip flexors, in below your pelvis. Move at about 1 inch per second while trying to relax the muscle.

4. If you roll over a spot that is particularly tender or sore, stay on it for 10-20 seconds while trying to relax the area, then move on. 

5. Ensure that you are only feeling a gentle discomfort, and not a serious or "sharp" pain.

exercises.name: Lacrosse Ball Lat Release
exercises.description:
1. Don't roll over any bony protrusions of the ribs or any joints, keep the ball on the muscle. 

2. Lie on your side, and place the ball just behind your armpit and below the shoulder - using your bodyweight, apply gentle pressure to the ball. 

3. Gently work your way around the lats, in behind your shoulder, and behind your ribs. Move at about 1 inch per second while trying to relax the muscle.

4. If you roll over a spot that is particularly tender or sore, stay on it for 10-20 seconds while trying to relax the area, then move on. 

5. Ensure that you are only feeling a gentle discomfort, and not a serious or "sharp" pain.

exercises.name: Lacrosse Ball Neck Release
exercises.description:
1. Don't roll over any bony protrusions of the neck or any joints, keep the ball on the muscle. 

2. Lie on your back, and place the ball just behind your neck and below the skull - using your bodyweight, apply gentle pressure to the ball. 

3. Gently work your way around the neck, in below your skull. Move at about 1 inch per second while trying to relax the muscle.

4. If you roll over a spot that is particularly tender or sore, stay on it for 10-20 seconds while trying to relax the area, then move on. 

5. Ensure that you are only feeling a gentle discomfort, and not a serious or "sharp" pain.

exercises.name: Lacrosse Ball Quad Release
exercises.description:
1. Don't roll over any bony protrusions of the ribs or any joints, keep the ball on the muscle. 

2. Start lying on your chest, and place the ball on the front of your thigh, just above the knee - using your bodyweight, apply gentle pressure to the ball. 

3. Gently work your way around the quad, in above your knee. Move at about 1 inch per second while trying to relax the muscle.

4. If you roll over a spot that is particularly tender or sore, stay on it for 10-20 seconds while trying to relax the area, then move on. 

5. Ensure that you are only feeling a gentle discomfort, and not a serious or "sharp" pain.

exercises.name: Lacrosse Ball Wrist Extensor Release
exercises.description:
1. Don't roll over any bony protrusions of the wrist or any joints, keep the ball on the muscle. 

2. Start kneeling, and place the ball on the outside of your forearm, just below the elbow - using your bodyweight, apply gentle pressure to the ball. 

3. Gently work your way around the extensors, in belowyour elbow, and above your wrist. Move at about 1 inch per second while trying to relax the muscle.

4. If you roll over a spot that is particularly tender or sore, stay on it for 10-20 seconds while trying to relax the area, then move on. 

5. Ensure that you are only feeling a gentle discomfort, and not a serious or "sharp" pain.

exercises.name: Lacrosse Ball Wrist Flexor Release
exercises.description:
1. Don't roll over any bony protrusions of the wrist or any joints, keep the ball on the muscle. 

2. Start kneeling, and place the ball on the inside of your forearm, just below the elbow - using your bodyweight, apply gentle pressure to the ball. 

3. Gently work your way around the flexors, in below your elbow, and above your wrist. Move at about 1 inch per second while trying to relax the muscle.

4. If you roll over a spot that is particularly tender or sore, stay on it for 10-20 seconds while trying to relax the area, then move on. 

5. Ensure that you are only feeling a gentle discomfort, and not a serious or "sharp" pain.
